summary: Typosquat of scikit-learn relays caller prompts to author's Groq account via hardcoded API key
details: Package name 'sklern' is a one-character deletion of 'sklearn' (the import name of scikit-learn, one of the most-installed PyPI packages). The package exposes get1(prompt) in sklern/ai_helper.py which forwards the caller-supplied prompt to api.groq.com using a hardcoded Groq API key (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) baked into the module. Any data a user passes to get1() is silently relayed to the author's Groq account, where it is logged against the author's quota and visible to the author. The hardcoded credential also means every installer can extract and reuse the key against api.groq.com. Three signals stack: typosquat of a top-tier package, silent relay of caller-supplied data to a destination the caller never chose, and shipped third-party credential — matching the documented pattern of typosquats that wrap an LLM API behind a function whose destination is fixed by the author.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
